rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Supplemental Responses To For Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 13, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANT COUNTY OF SAN MATEO'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Supplemental Responses To Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS, THE COUNTY OF SAN MATEO AND CELIA MORENO' (COLLECTIVELY, "DEFENDANTS"), MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO FILE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO CERTAIN FORM INTERROGATORIES, FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND SANCTION APPEARS TO BE UNOPPOSED AND, ACCORDINGLY WILL BE GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: 1 PLAINTIFF, WITHIN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, SHALL SERVE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 202.1,204.3,204.6&208.1 SETTING FORTH DATES, IDENTITIES OF INDIVIDUALS TO WHICH SHE MAKES REFERENCE AND SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFYING DOCUMENTS REFERRED TO IN SUCH RESPONSES. TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO PROVIDE SUCH INFORMATION AS TO DATES, INDIVIDUALS IN CONNECTION WITH ANY OF THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES OR DOCUMENTS SHE SHOULD SO STATE THE REASONS WHY SHE IS UNABLE TO SUPPLY THE INFORMATION AND, WITHOUT A SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE, WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM RELYING UPON THE ALLEGATIONS OF THAT PARTICULAR FORM INTERROGATORY AT THE TIME OF TRIAL. 2 PLAINTIFF, WITHIN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, SHALL SERVE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 210.2, 210.3,210.5,212.4,212.5 WHICH WILL STATE WITH SPECIFICITY THE AMOUNT OF HER CLAIM FOR LOSS OF BENEFITS, INCOME AND EARNING CAPACITY; DETAILS OF ALL SUBSEQUENT EMPLOYMENT BOTH AS TO IDENTITY OF EMPLOYER(S), DATES OF EMPLOYMENT AND PAYMENTS RECEIVED. IN THIS REGARD PLAINTIFF WILL PRODUCE WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER ALL DOCUMENTS WHICH REFLECT SUCH PAYMENTS. 3 MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE ASSESSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000.00 WITH PAYMENT TO BE MADE WITHIN TEN(10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER AND SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON JANUARY 13, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KARMA QUICK VS. JONATHAN FRIED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, January 13, 2014, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT KARMA QUICK, DUSTIN COLLIER, DREW TETI AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The First Amended Cross-Complaint fails to comply with CRC 2.112(4). Given that defect, it is unnecessary to rule specifically on the various other grounds. However, any amended complaint should more clearly state facts supporting a legal basis for the relief sought against the defendants to whom it is addressed. Neither a statute of limitations nor a form of relief is itself a basis for a cause of action. Fraud-based claims must be stated with particularity against each defendant to whom they are addressed. If claims are to be asserted against either Mr. Collier or Mr. Teti, cross-complainant must either comply with the procedural requirements of section 1714.10 or plead non-conclusory facts that would base a claim against them on something other than their conduct in the course of their representation of Ms. Quick in connection with this dispute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/pjb

rulings.Case Title: DGP ASSOCIATES LP VS. AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant American Guarantee And Liability Insurance'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Its Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Request For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two And Documents Responsive To Its Subpoena To Greenspan Co./Adjusters International; Request For In Camera Review (Ccp 2031.010-2031.510)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 13, 2014, Line 5: Defendant American Guarantee And Liability Insurance Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Its Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Request For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two And Documents Responsive To Its Subpoena To Greenspan Co./Adjusters International; Request For In Camera Review (CCP 2031.010-2031.510) PART 2 OF 2: The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO ITS SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET TWO, REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ON AND TWO AND DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO ITS SUBPOENA TO GREENSPAN CO/ADJUSTERS INTERNATIONAL IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:, 1 COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF, DGP ASSOCIATES, WILL DELIVER TO THE COURT COPIES OF DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO PRODUCTION REQUESTS NOS. 13,17, 27 AND 32, AS WELL AS THE GREENSPAN CO. DOCUMENTS TO WHICH IT ASSERTS A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW FOR DETERMINATION AS TO WHETHER OR NOT THE DOCUMENTS ENTITLED TO A PRIVILEGE CLAIM AND, IF SO WHETHER OR NOT THAT CLAIM HAS BEEN WAIVED BY REASON OF ITS DISCLOSURE TO A THIRD PARTY. 2 PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE MOTION TO COMPEL IDENTIFICATION OF DOCUMENTS AS REQUESTED IN SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 28-37 ARE OVERRULED. WITHIN FIFTEEN DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER PLAINTIFF WILL PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH FURTHER RESPONSES WHICH NEED NOT BE AN EXHAUSTIVE LISTING, BUT WILL BE THOSE UPON WHICH IT PRINCIPALLY INTENDS TO RELY IN SUPPORTING ITS CLAIM OF THE 12 REPRESENTATIONS 3 PLAINTIFF WILL PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH DOCUMENTS SUFFICIENT TO SHOW ALL EXPENSES INCURRED BY IT IN CONNECTION WITH THE OWNERSHIP OF THE CABOT, HAYWARD AND LINCOLN PROPERTIES FROM OCTOBER 2010 TO THE PRESENT. 4 DEFENDANT'S REQUEST THE DGP PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT CONTENDS THAT DPG IS SEEKING A DOUBLE RECOVERY, THE IDENTIFICATION OF THE INDIVIDUAL PARTNERS WHO MAY PROFIT FROM THIS DOUBLE RECOVERY IS IRRELEVANT TO THE ISSUES IN THE LITIGATION. 5 NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON JANUARY 13, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: FERDINAND AUSTRIA et al VS. FRED W. SCHWINN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, January 13, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFF FERDINAND AUSTRIA, CRAIG MILLER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Denied. Plaintiffs' evidence is sufficient to establish a prima facie claim under Penal Code § 632. It is undisputed the that Defendant electronically recorded the parties' conversation on January 17, 2012. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Plaintiffs' Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 7.) The Declarations of Plaintiffs Sperandio, Miller, and Weisberg demonstrate an objective reasonable expectation that the conversation would be confidential and that they did not consent to Defendant's electronic recording of the conversation. (UMF 2-11; see also Flanagan v. Flanagan (2002) 27 Cal.4th 766, 768, 774-776.) Consequently, the burden shifts to Defendant. Defendant's declaration, indulging all reasonable inferences in Defendant's favor, demonstrates a triable issue of fact regarding whether the Plaintiffs, by their conduct and failure to object, effectively knew the meeting was being recorded and whether they consented to having the conversation electronically recorded. (See Declaration of Schwinn 4-6, the objections to which are overruled.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/pjb

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THE COURT WILL ORDER THAT THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT GO FORWARD ON JANUARY 15, 2014 COMMENCING AT 9:00 A; DEFENDANT'S VEHICLE INSPECTION WILL TAKE PLACE COMMENCING AT 9:00 AM ON JANUARY 16, 2014 AND THE DEPOSITION OF FORD'S PMK WILL COMMENCE TWO HOURS AFTER THE COMPLETION OF THE VEHICLE INSPECTION AND CONTINUE FROM DAY TO DAY UNTIL COMPLETED. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. COUNSEL FOR FORD SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR AGREEMENT AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON JANUARY 13, 2014. IF PRIOR TO THE HEARING ON JANUARY 13, 2014 COUNSEL FOR THE PARTIES CAN REACH AGREEMENT ON A SCHEDULE FOR THE DEPOSITIONS AND VEHICLE INSPECTION WHICH IS CONSISTENT WITH THE SEQUENCING OF THE DISCOVERY AS SET FORTH IN THE COURT'S ORDER, THE COURT IS PREPARED TO AMEND THE ORDER TO BE CONSISTENT WITH SUCH AGREEMENT. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SUHILA GEBRAIL et al VS. DANIEL R. RIELLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Of A Separate Trial And To Transfer Action And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, January 13, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANT JUAN VELASQUEZ Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Of A Separate Trial And To Transfer Action And Monetary Sanctions Denied without prejudice. It is premature to determine that separate trials will necessarily be appropriate if the two defendants remain in this case. CCP sec. 1048 on which this motion is premised permits separate trials, but is not a proper mechanism to raise misjoinder of parties. Therefore, the Court cannot determine whether venue would be proper if misjoinder were determined. This decision is, however, without prejudice to raising misjoinder of the defendants pursuant to CCP sec. 430.10(d), if that remains an available option, or in some other procedurally proper way. All requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/pjb
